title: Wire energy.get_energy_curve through CacheStore (L2)
description: Layer the sidecar cache underneath the existing in-process energy._cache. Reads check L1 (in-process), then L2 (CacheStore), then compute. Writes populate both. Existing _cache stays as a thin LRU.
acceptanceCriteria: analysis/energy.py:get_energy_curve(content, db, n_points=50) returns identical values to v0 on cache miss; same values on warm cache.
Warm-cache call latency < 1 ms (no ANLZ parse).
Cold-cache call still parses ANLZ and populates CacheStore.
ANLZ-missing tracks return None and store the -1 sentinel - subsequent calls also return None without re-reading.
tests/test_energy.py adds cases for L2 hit, L1 hit, miss-and-populate, and missing-ANLZ sentinel.
conftest autouse fixture clears both energy._cache AND CacheStore between tests (so cached results don't leak across cases).

steps.details: Add a module-level _cache_store: CacheStore | None = None. Provide energy.set_cache_store(store) called from serve/deps.py lifespan after CacheStore is opened.

Order: (1) check _cache (L1) by (content.ID, n_points) - return on hit; (2) if _cache_store, get expected_anlz_mtime = get_anlz_mtime(content, db); call _cache_store.get_energy_curve(content.ID, expected_anlz_mtime) - if data: populate L1 and return; if MISSING sentinel: populate L1 with None and return None; (3) compute curve via existing ANLZ parse; (4) write to L1 and L2 (with anlz_mtime).

CLAUDE.md notes energy._cache is keyed by (content.ID, n_points). The CacheStore.energy_curve table stores n_points and the full BLOB. Reader checks: if cached n_points == requested n_points, return; else recompute (rare - only when callers explicitly ask for non-default).

tests/test_energy.py: (a) miss-and-populate writes to both L1 and L2 (assert CacheStore row exists); (b) L1 hit short-circuits (no DB read); (c) L2 hit populates L1 (assert L1 dict updates); (d) sentinel after first None result causes subsequent calls to return None without ANLZ access (use mock to assert read_anlz_file is not re-invoked).

tests/conftest.py: extend the autouse fixture to also clear CacheStore. For unit tests use an in-memory CacheStore (sqlite3.connect(':memory:')) bound to energy via set_cache_store. Reset between tests by closing + reopening.

technicalNotes: energy._cache is the existing LRU. After this task it's an L1 cache (small, hot). Sidecar is L2 (large, durable).
If energy.set_cache_store is never called (e.g., in unit tests that don't use CacheStore), get_energy_curve falls back to L1+compute - no breakage.
